Kagoshima Prefectural Industrial School
Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Industrial School
Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima High School First Division
Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School | type = [[:en:State school|Public school]] | founder = Kagoshima Prefecture | established = 1908 | motto = Diligence, Creativity, Sincerity | gender = [[:en:Mixed-sex education|Co-educational]] | curriculum = {{ill|Full-time education|ja|高等学校#全日制|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Full-time education|qq=}} | system = Year-based | departments = See below | school_code = 46107F | postalcode = 890-0014 | location = 2-57-1 Kusamuta, Kagoshima City, Kagoshima Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|31|36|30.5|N|130|32|18.4|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | website = {{Official website}} }} [[File:Dai-entotsu-kagoshima-kougyo.jpg|200px|thumb|right|Large Chimney]] '''Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School''' (鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校, ''Kagoshima Kenritsu Kagoshima Kōgyō Kōtō Gakkō'') is a {{ill|prefectural|ja|県立|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/prefectural|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 公立}} [[:en:Higher Technical Examination Programme|technical high school]] located in [[:en:Kagoshima|Kagoshima City]], Kagoshima Prefecture. Commonly known as '''Rokkō''' (鹿工). == Overview == Founded in 1908 as Kagoshima District Industrial Apprentice School. Like many technical high schools in Japan, it is co-educational, though about 90% of the students are male, with over 20,000 alumni. Its symbol is a large brick chimney built in 1920, featured in the school song and designated as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Property|qq=}} by the [[:en:Agency for Cultural Affairs|Agency for Cultural Affairs]] in 2004.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/117074 Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School Large Chimney] - Cultural Heritage Online Locally, it's often simply referred to as "Kōgyō" (工業) to distinguish it from {{ill|Kagoshima High School|ja|鹿児島高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima High School|qq=}}, known as "Kakō" (鹿高). === Educational Program === * First-year students are grouped by categories, while second and third years are divided into specialized courses ("kei"). * Students choose their specialization at the end of the first year. ; Industrial Category I * Mechatronics * Electrical Engineering * Information Technology * Industrial Chemistry ; Industrial Category II * Architecture * Civil Engineering * Interior Design === School Motto === * Diligence * Creativity * Sincerity == School Song == * Lyrics by Matsujirō Katayama * Music by Kyūhachi Maeda == History == * 1908: Established as Kagoshima District Industrial Apprentice School. * 1919: Renamed Kagoshima Prefectural Industrial School. * 1920: Opening of Kagoshima Prefectural Industrial School; establishment of Mechanical, Architectural, and Furniture departments. * 1925: Furniture Department abolished. * 1927: Renamed Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Industrial School. * 1939: Electrical Department established. * 1948: Renamed Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima High School First Division following educational reforms. * 1949: Reorganized as Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School. (Additional historical details follow the same pattern.) == Club Activities == ; Baseball * Achieved its first appearance at [[:en:Koshien|Koshien]] in 2006, reaching the semifinals. ; Soccer * Participated in the [[:en:All Japan High School Soccer Tournament|All Japan High School Soccer Tournament]] four times. ; Rugby * Eleven-time participant in the [[:en:National High School Rugby Tournament|National High School Rugby Tournament]]. ; Archery * Won the national archery tournament in 2007. == Access == * [[:en:Kagoshima City Transportation Bureau|Kagoshima City Transportation Bureau]] Bus No. 5 - "Kōgyō Kōkō-mae" bus stop. * "Gokoku Jinja-mae" bus stop served by city and regional buses. == Notable Alumni == ; Politics * Toshio Sueyoshi - Former Mayor of Kagoshima ; Sports * Munenori Kawasaki - Former professional baseball player (SoftBank Hawks, MLB) * Yūsaku Kuwazuru - Japanese national rugby player (Additional alumni listed accordingly.) == References == == External Links == * {{Official website}} * [http://rokkohob-org.easy-magic.com/user/ Alumni Association Website] * [http://rrfc.jp/ Rugby Club Alumni Association] * [http://rokkou-boxing.com/ Boxing Club Alumni Association] {{Navboxes |title= Club Achievements |list1= {{National High School Archery Tournament Boys' Champions}} {{National High School Archery Tournament Girls' Champions}} }} {{translated page|ja|鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校|version=102422747|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Technical high schools in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:%E9%B9%BF%E5%85%90%E5%B3%B6%E7%9C%8C%E7%AB%8B%E9%B9%BF%E5%85%90%E5%B3%B6%E5%B7%A5%E6%A5%AD%E9%AB%98%E7%AD%89%E5%AD%A6%E6%A0%A1]] [[sn:Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School]] [[Category:全国高等学校サッカー選手権大会出場校]] [[Category:全国高等学校ラグビーフットボール大会出場校]] [[Category:全国高等学校選抜ラグビーフットボール大会出場校]] [[Category:全国高等学校野球選手権大会出場校]] [[Category:全国高等学校駅伝競走大会男子の部出場校]] [[Category:Technical high schools in Japan]] [[Category:選抜高等学校野球大会出場校]] [[Category:鹿児島市の公立高等学校]] [[ja:鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校]]

revision.comment: Merge [[Category:選抜高等学校野球大会出場校]] into [[Category:High Schools in the National High School Baseball Championship]] via bot
revision.text:
{{Unreferenced|date=April 2021}} {{Infobox Japanese school | name = Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School | image = no | former_name = Kagoshima District Industrial Apprentice School
Kagoshima Prefectural Industrial School
Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Industrial School
Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima High School First Division
Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School | type = [[:en:State school|Public school]] | founder = Kagoshima Prefecture | established = 1908 | motto = Diligence, Creativity, Sincerity | gender = [[:en:Mixed-sex education|Co-educational]] | curriculum = {{ill|Full-time education|ja|高等学校#全日制|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Full-time education|qq=}} | system = Year-based | departments = See below | school_code = 46107F | postalcode = 890-0014 | location = 2-57-1 Kusamuta, Kagoshima City, Kagoshima Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|31|36|30.5|N|130|32|18.4|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | website = {{Official website}} }} [[File:Dai-entotsu-kagoshima-kougyo.jpg|200px|thumb|right|Large Chimney]] '''Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School''' (鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校, ''Kagoshima Kenritsu Kagoshima Kōgyō Kōtō Gakkō'') is a {{ill|prefectural|ja|県立|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/prefectural|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 公立}} [[:en:Higher Technical Examination Programme|technical high school]] located in [[:en:Kagoshima|Kagoshima City]], Kagoshima Prefecture. Commonly known as '''Rokkō''' (鹿工). == Overview == Founded in 1908 as Kagoshima District Industrial Apprentice School. Like many technical high schools in Japan, it is co-educational, though about 90% of the students are male, with over 20,000 alumni. Its symbol is a large brick chimney built in 1920, featured in the school song and designated as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Property|qq=}} by the [[:en:Agency for Cultural Affairs|Agency for Cultural Affairs]] in 2004.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/117074 Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School Large Chimney] - Cultural Heritage Online Locally, it's often simply referred to as "Kōgyō" (工業) to distinguish it from {{ill|Kagoshima High School|ja|鹿児島高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima High School|qq=}}, known as "Kakō" (鹿高). === Educational Program === * First-year students are grouped by categories, while second and third years are divided into specialized courses ("kei"). * Students choose their specialization at the end of the first year. ; Industrial Category I * Mechatronics * Electrical Engineering * Information Technology * Industrial Chemistry ; Industrial Category II * Architecture * Civil Engineering * Interior Design === School Motto === * Diligence * Creativity * Sincerity == School Song == * Lyrics by Matsujirō Katayama * Music by Kyūhachi Maeda == History == * 1908: Established as Kagoshima District Industrial Apprentice School. * 1919: Renamed Kagoshima Prefectural Industrial School. * 1920: Opening of Kagoshima Prefectural Industrial School; establishment of Mechanical, Architectural, and Furniture departments. * 1925: Furniture Department abolished. * 1927: Renamed Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Industrial School. * 1939: Electrical Department established. * 1948: Renamed Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima High School First Division following educational reforms. * 1949: Reorganized as Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School. (Additional historical details follow the same pattern.) == Club Activities == ; Baseball * Achieved its first appearance at [[:en:Koshien|Koshien]] in 2006, reaching the semifinals. ; Soccer * Participated in the [[:en:All Japan High School Soccer Tournament|All Japan High School Soccer Tournament]] four times. ; Rugby * Eleven-time participant in the [[:en:National High School Rugby Tournament|National High School Rugby Tournament]]. ; Archery * Won the national archery tournament in 2007. == Access == * [[:en:Kagoshima City Transportation Bureau|Kagoshima City Transportation Bureau]] Bus No. 5 - "Kōgyō Kōkō-mae" bus stop. * "Gokoku Jinja-mae" bus stop served by city and regional buses. == Notable Alumni == ; Politics * Toshio Sueyoshi - Former Mayor of Kagoshima ; Sports * Munenori Kawasaki - Former professional baseball player (SoftBank Hawks, MLB) * Yūsaku Kuwazuru - Japanese national rugby player (Additional alumni listed accordingly.) == References == == External Links == * {{Official website}} * [http://rokkohob-org.easy-magic.com/user/ Alumni Association Website] * [http://rrfc.jp/ Rugby Club Alumni Association] * [http://rokkou-boxing.com/ Boxing Club Alumni Association] {{Navboxes |title= Club Achievements |list1= {{National High School Archery Tournament Boys' Champions}} {{National High School Archery Tournament Girls' Champions}} }} {{translated page|ja|鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校|version=102422747|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Technical high schools in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:%E9%B9%BF%E5%85%90%E5%B3%B6%E7%9C%8C%E7%AB%8B%E9%B9%BF%E5%85%90%E5%B3%B6%E5%B7%A5%E6%A5%AD%E9%AB%98%E7%AD%89%E5%AD%A6%E6%A0%A1]] [[sn:Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School]] [[Category:High Schools in National Soccer Championship]] [[Category:High Schools in National Rugby Championship]] [[Category:全国高等学校選抜ラグビーフットボール大会出場校]] [[Category:全国高等学校野球選手権大会出場校]] [[Category:全国高等学校駅伝競走大会男子の部出場校]] [[Category:Technical high schools in Japan]] [[Category:High Schools in the National High School Baseball Championship]] [[Category:Public High Schools in Kagoshima City]] [[ja:鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=April 2021}} {{Infobox Japanese school | name = Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School | image = no | former_name = Kagoshima District Industrial Apprentice School
Kagoshima Prefectural Industrial School
Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Industrial School
Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima High School First Division
Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School | type = [[:en:State school|Public school]] | founder = Kagoshima Prefecture | established = 1908 | motto = Diligence, Creativity, Sincerity | gender = [[:en:Mixed-sex education|Co-educational]] | curriculum = {{ill|Full-time education|ja|高等学校#全日制|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Full-time education|qq=}} | system = Year-based | departments = See below | school_code = 46107F | postalcode = 890-0014 | location = 2-57-1 Kusamuta, Kagoshima City, Kagoshima Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|31|36|30.5|N|130|32|18.4|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | website = {{Official website}} }} [[File:Dai-entotsu-kagoshima-kougyo.jpg|200px|thumb|right|Large Chimney]] '''Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School''' (鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校, ''Kagoshima Kenritsu Kagoshima Kōgyō Kōtō Gakkō'') is a {{ill|prefectural|ja|県立|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/prefectural|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 公立}} [[:en:Higher Technical Examination Programme|technical high school]] located in [[:en:Kagoshima|Kagoshima City]], Kagoshima Prefecture. Commonly known as '''Rokkō''' (鹿工). == Overview == Founded in 1908 as Kagoshima District Industrial Apprentice School. Like many technical high schools in Japan, it is co-educational, though about 90% of the students are male, with over 20,000 alumni. Its symbol is a large brick chimney built in 1920, featured in the school song and designated as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Property|qq=}} by the [[:en:Agency for Cultural Affairs|Agency for Cultural Affairs]] in 2004.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/117074 Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School Large Chimney] - Cultural Heritage Online Locally, it's often simply referred to as "Kōgyō" (工業) to distinguish it from {{ill|Kagoshima High School|ja|鹿児島高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima High School|qq=}}, known as "Kakō" (鹿高). === Educational Program === * First-year students are grouped by categories, while second and third years are divided into specialized courses ("kei"). * Students choose their specialization at the end of the first year. ; Industrial Category I * Mechatronics * Electrical Engineering * Information Technology * Industrial Chemistry ; Industrial Category II * Architecture * Civil Engineering * Interior Design === School Motto === * Diligence * Creativity * Sincerity == School Song == * Lyrics by Matsujirō Katayama * Music by Kyūhachi Maeda == History == * 1908: Established as Kagoshima District Industrial Apprentice School. * 1919: Renamed Kagoshima Prefectural Industrial School. * 1920: Opening of Kagoshima Prefectural Industrial School; establishment of Mechanical, Architectural, and Furniture departments. * 1925: Furniture Department abolished. * 1927: Renamed Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Industrial School. * 1939: Electrical Department established. * 1948: Renamed Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima High School First Division following educational reforms. * 1949: Reorganized as Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School. (Additional historical details follow the same pattern.) == Club Activities == ; Baseball * Achieved its first appearance at [[:en:Koshien|Koshien]] in 2006, reaching the semifinals. ; Soccer * Participated in the [[:en:All Japan High School Soccer Tournament|All Japan High School Soccer Tournament]] four times. ; Rugby * Eleven-time participant in the [[:en:National High School Rugby Tournament|National High School Rugby Tournament]]. ; Archery * Won the national archery tournament in 2007. == Access == * [[:en:Kagoshima City Transportation Bureau|Kagoshima City Transportation Bureau]] Bus No. 5 - "Kōgyō Kōkō-mae" bus stop. * "Gokoku Jinja-mae" bus stop served by city and regional buses. == Notable Alumni == ; Politics * Toshio Sueyoshi - Former Mayor of Kagoshima ; Sports * Munenori Kawasaki - Former professional baseball player (SoftBank Hawks, MLB) * Yūsaku Kuwazuru - Japanese national rugby player (Additional alumni listed accordingly.) == References == == External Links == * {{Official website}} * [http://rokkohob-org.easy-magic.com/user/ Alumni Association Website] * [http://rrfc.jp/ Rugby Club Alumni Association] * [http://rokkou-boxing.com/ Boxing Club Alumni Association] {{Navboxes |title= Club Achievements |list1= {{National High School Archery Tournament Boys' Champions}} {{National High School Archery Tournament Girls' Champions}} }} {{translated page|ja|鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校|version=102422747|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Technical high schools in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:%E9%B9%BF%E5%85%90%E5%B3%B6%E7%9C%8C%E7%AB%8B%E9%B9%BF%E5%85%90%E5%B3%B6%E5%B7%A5%E6%A5%AD%E9%AB%98%E7%AD%89%E5%AD%A6%E6%A0%A1]] [[sn:Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School]] [[Category:High Schools in National Soccer Championship]] [[Category:High Schools in National Rugby Championship]] [[Category:Participating schools in the All-Japan High School Rugby Selection Tournament]] [[Category:Participating schools in the All-Japan High School Baseball Championship]] [[Category:Participating schools in the Boys Division of the All-Japan High School Ekiden Championship]] [[Category:Technical high schools in Japan]] [[Category:High Schools in the National High School Baseball Championship]] [[Category:Public High Schools in Kagoshima City]] [[ja:鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校]] [[Category:paes with no wikidata]]

Politics * Toshio Sueyoshi - Former Mayor of Kagoshima ; Sports * Munenori Kawasaki - Former professional baseball player (SoftBank Hawks, MLB) * Yūsaku Kuwazuru - Japanese national rugby player (Additional alumni listed accordingly.) == References == == External Links == * {{Official website}} * [http://rokkohob-org.easy-magic.com/user/ Alumni Association Website] * [http://rrfc.jp/ Rugby Club Alumni Association] * [http://rokkou-boxing.com/ Boxing Club Alumni Association] {{Navboxes |title= Club Achievements |list1= {{National High School Archery Tournament Boys' Champions}} {{National High School Archery Tournament Girls' Champions}} }} {{translated page|ja|鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校|version=102422747|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Technical high schools in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:E9%B9%BF%E5%85%90%E5%B3%B6%E7%9C%8C%E7%AB%8B%E9%B9%BF%E5%85%90%E5%B3%B6%E5%B7%A5%E6%A5%AD%E9%AB%98%E7%AD%89%E5%AD%A6%E6%A0%A1]] [[sn:Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School]] [[Category:High Schools in National Soccer Championship]] [[Category:High Schools in National Rugby Championship]] [[Category:Participating schools in the All-Japan High School Rugby Selection Tournament]] [[Category:Participating schools in the All-Japan High School Baseball Championship]] [[Category:Participating schools in the Boys Division of the All-Japan High School Ekiden Championship]] [[Category:Technical high schools in Japan]] [[Category:High Schools in the National High School Baseball Championship]] [[Category:Public High Schools in Kagoshima City]] [[ja:鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校]] [[Category:Pages with no Wikidata]] [[Category:Pages with no Wikidata]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11676827]] Wikidata name: Kagoshima Technical High School [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:16, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:54, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Unreferenced|date=April 2021}} {{Infobox Japanese school | name = Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School | image = no | former_name = Kagoshima District Industrial Apprentice School
Kagoshima Prefectural Industrial School
Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Industrial School
Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima High School First Division
Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School | type = [[:en:State school|Public school]] | founder = Kagoshima Prefecture | established = 1908 | motto = Diligence, Creativity, Sincerity | gender = [[:en:Mixed-sex education|Co-educational]] | curriculum = {{ill|Full-time education|ja|高等学校#全日制|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Full-time education|qq=}} | system = Year-based | departments = See below | school_code = 46107F | postalcode = 890-0014 | location = 2-57-1 Kusamuta, Kagoshima City, Kagoshima Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|31|36|30.5|N|130|32|18.4|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | website = {{Official website}} }} [[File:Dai-entotsu-kagoshima-kougyo.jpg|200px|thumb|right|Large Chimney]] '''Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School''' (鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校, ''Kagoshima Kenritsu Kagoshima Kōgyō Kōtō Gakkō'') is a {{ill|prefectural|ja|県立|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/prefectural|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 公立}} [[:en:Higher Technical Examination Programme|technical high school]] located in [[:en:Kagoshima|Kagoshima City]], Kagoshima Prefecture. Commonly known as '''Rokkō''' (鹿工). == Overview == Founded in 1908 as Kagoshima District Industrial Apprentice School. Like many technical high schools in Japan, it is co-educational, though about 90% of the students are male, with over 20,000 alumni. Its symbol is a large brick chimney built in 1920, featured in the school song and designated as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Property|qq=}} by the [[:en:Agency for Cultural Affairs|Agency for Cultural Affairs]] in 2004.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/117074 Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School Large Chimney] - Cultural Heritage Online Locally, it's often simply referred to as "Kōgyō" (工業) to distinguish it from {{ill|Kagoshima High School|ja|鹿児島高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima High School|qq=}}, known as "Kakō" (鹿高). === Educational Program === * First-year students are grouped by categories, while second and third years are divided into specialized courses ("kei"). * Students choose their specialization at the end of the first year. ; Industrial Category I * Mechatronics * Electrical Engineering * Information Technology * Industrial Chemistry ; Industrial Category II * Architecture * Civil Engineering * Interior Design === School Motto === * Diligence * Creativity * Sincerity == School Song == * Lyrics by Matsujirō Katayama * Music by Kyūhachi Maeda == History == * 1908: Established as Kagoshima District Industrial Apprentice School. * 1919: Renamed Kagoshima Prefectural Industrial School. * 1920: Opening of Kagoshima Prefectural Industrial School; establishment of Mechanical, Architectural, and Furniture departments. * 1925: Furniture Department abolished. * 1927: Renamed Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Industrial School. * 1939: Electrical Department established. * 1948: Renamed Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima High School First Division following educational reforms. * 1949: Reorganized as Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School. (Additional historical details follow the same pattern.) == Club Activities == ; Baseball * Achieved its first appearance at [[:en:Koshien|Koshien]] in 2006, reaching the semifinals. ; Soccer * Participated in the [[:en:All Japan High School Soccer Tournament|All Japan High School Soccer Tournament]] four times. ; Rugby * Eleven-time participant in the [[:en:National High School Rugby Tournament|National High School Rugby Tournament]]. ; Archery * Won the national archery tournament in 2007. == Access == * [[:en:Kagoshima City Transportation Bureau|Kagoshima City Transportation Bureau]] Bus No. 5 - "Kōgyō Kōkō-mae" bus stop. * "Gokoku Jinja-mae" bus stop served by city and regional buses. == Notable Alumni == ; Politics * Toshio Sueyoshi - Former Mayor of Kagoshima ; Sports * Munenori Kawasaki - Former professional baseball player (SoftBank Hawks, MLB) * Yūsaku Kuwazuru - Japanese national rugby player (Additional alumni listed accordingly.) == References == == External Links == * {{Official website}} * [http://rokkohob-org.easy-magic.com/user/ Alumni Association Website] * [http://rrfc.jp/ Rugby Club Alumni Association] * [http://rokkou-boxing.com/ Boxing Club Alumni Association] {{Navboxes |title= Club Achievements |list1= {{National High School Archery Tournament Boys' Champions}} {{National High School Archery Tournament Girls' Champions}} }} {{translated page|ja|鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校|version=102422747|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Technical high schools in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:E9%B9%BF%E5%85%90%E5%B3%B6%E7%9C%8C%E7%AB%8B%E9%B9%BF%E5%85%90%E5%B3%B6%E5%B7%A5%E6%A5%AD%E9%AB%98%E7%AD%89%E5%AD%A6%E6%A0%A1]] [[sn:Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Technical High School]] [[Category:High Schools in National Soccer Championship]] [[Category:High Schools in National Rugby Championship]] [[Category:Participating schools in the All-Japan High School Rugby Selection Tournament]] [[Category:Participating schools in the All-Japan High School Baseball Championship]] [[Category:Participating schools in the Boys Division of the All-Japan High School Ekiden Championship]] [[Category:Technical high schools in Japan]] [[Category:High Schools in the National High School Baseball Championship]] [[Category:Public High Schools in Kagoshima City]] [[ja:鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校]] [[Category:Pages with no Wikidata]] [[Category:Pages with no Wikidata]]

(Additional historical details follow the same pattern.) == Club Activities == ; Baseball * Achieved its first appearance at [[:en:Koshien|Koshien]] in 2006, reaching the semifinals. ; Soccer * Participated in the [[:en:All Japan High School Soccer Tournament|All Japan High School Soccer Tournament]] four times. ; Rugby * Eleven-time participant in the [[:en:National High School Rugby Tournament|National High School Rugby Tournament]]. ; Archery * Won the national archery tournament in 2007. == Access == * [[:en:Kagoshima City Transportation Bureau|Kagoshima City Transportation Bureau]] Bus No. 5 - "Kōgyō Kōkō-mae" bus stop. * "Gokoku Jinja-mae" bus stop served by city and regional buses. == Notable Alumni == ; Politics * Toshio Sueyoshi - Former Mayor of Kagoshima ; Sports * Munenori Kawasaki - Former professional baseball player (SoftBank Hawks, MLB) * Yūsaku Kuwazuru - Japanese national rugby player (Additional alumni listed accordingly.) == References == == External Links == * {{Official website}} * [http://rokkohob-org.easy-magic.com/user/ Alumni Association Website] * [http://rrfc.jp/ Rugby Club Alumni Association] * [http://rokkou-boxing.com/ Boxing Club Alumni Association] {{Navboxes |title= Club Achievements |list1= {{National High School Archery Tournament Boys' Champions}} {{National High School Archery Tournament Girls' Champions}} }} {{translated page|ja|鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校|version=102422747|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Technical high schools in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[sn:E9%B9%BF%E5%85%90%E5%B3%B6%E7%9C%8C%E7%AB%8B%E9%B9%BF%E5%85%90%E5%B3%B6%E5%B7%A5%E6%A5%AD%E9%AB%98%E7%AD%89%E5%AD%A6%E6%A0%A1]] [[sn:Kagoshima Technical High School]] [[Category:High Schools in National Soccer Championship]] [[Category:High Schools in National Rugby Championship]] [[Category:Participating schools in the All-Japan High School Rugby Selection Tournament]] [[Category:Participating schools in the All-Japan High School Baseball Championship]] [[Category:Participating schools in the Boys Division of the All-Japan High School Ekiden Championship]] [[Category:Technical high schools in Japan]] [[Category:High Schools in the National High School Baseball Championship]] [[Category:Public High Schools in Kagoshima City]] [[ja:鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校]]

Its symbol is a large brick chimney built in 1920, featured in the school song and designated as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Property|qq=|1=Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579194}} by the [[:en:Agency for Cultural Affairs|Agency for Cultural Affairs]] in 2004.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/117074 Kagoshima Technical High School Large Chimney] - Cultural Heritage Online Locally, it's often simply referred to as "Kōgyō" (工業) to distinguish it from {{ill|Kagoshima High School|ja|鹿児島高等学校|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima High School|qq=|1=Kagoshima Academy|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677012}}, known as "Kakō" (鹿高). === Educational Program === * First-year students are grouped by categories, while second and third years are divided into specialized courses ("kei"). * Students choose their specialization at the end of the first year. ; Industrial Category I * Mechatronics * Electrical Engineering * Information Technology * Industrial Chemistry ; Industrial Category II * Architecture * Civil Engineering * Interior Design === School Motto === * Diligence * Creativity * Sincerity == School Song == * Lyrics by Matsujirō Katayama * Music by Kyūhachi Maeda == History == * 1908: Established as Kagoshima District Industrial Apprentice School. * 1919: Renamed Kagoshima Prefectural Industrial School. * 1920: Opening of Kagoshima Prefectural Industrial School; establishment of Mechanical, Architectural, and Furniture departments. * 1925: Furniture Department abolished. * 1927: Renamed Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Industrial School. * 1939: Electrical Department established. * 1948: Renamed Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima High School First Division following educational reforms. * 1949: Reorganized as Kagoshima Technical High School. (Additional historical details follow the same pattern.) == Club Activities == ; Baseball * Achieved its first appearance at [[:en:Koshien|Koshien]] in 2006, reaching the semifinals. ; Soccer * Participated in the [[:en:All Japan High School Soccer Tournament|All Japan High School Soccer Tournament]] four times. ; Rugby * Eleven-time participant in the [[:en:National High School Rugby Tournament|National High School Rugby Tournament]]. ; Archery * Won the national archery tournament in 2007. == Access == * [[:en:Kagoshima City Transportation Bureau|Kagoshima City Transportation Bureau]] Bus No. 5 - "Kōgyō Kōkō-mae" bus stop. * "Gokoku Jinja-mae" bus stop served by city and regional buses. == Notable Alumni == ; Politics * Toshio Sueyoshi - Former Mayor of Kagoshima ; Sports * Munenori Kawasaki - Former professional baseball player (SoftBank Hawks, MLB) * Yūsaku Kuwazuru - Japanese national rugby player (Additional alumni listed accordingly.) == References == == External Links == * {{Official website}} * [http://rokkohob-org.easy-magic.com/user/ Alumni Association Website] * [http://rrfc.jp/ Rugby Club Alumni Association] * [http://rokkou-boxing.com/ Boxing Club Alumni Association] {{Navboxes |title= Club Achievements |list1= {{National High School Archery Tournament Boys' Champions}} {{National High School Archery Tournament Girls' Champions}} }} {{translated page|ja|鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校|version=102422747|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Technical high schools in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[Category:High Schools in National Soccer Championship]] [[Category:High Schools in National Rugby Championship]] [[Category:Participating schools in the All-Japan High School Rugby Selection Tournament]] [[Category:Participating schools in the All-Japan High School Baseball Championship]] [[Category:Participating schools in the Boys Division of the All-Japan High School Ekiden Championship]] [[Category:Technical high schools in Japan]] [[Category:High Schools in the National High School Baseball Championship]] [[Category:Public High Schools in Kagoshima City]] [[ja:鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校]] {{wikidata link|Q11676827}}

Its symbol is a large brick chimney built in 1920, featured in the school song and designated as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Property|1=Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579194}} by the [[:en:Agency for Cultural Affairs|Agency for Cultural Affairs]] in 2004.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/117074 Kagoshima Technical High School Large Chimney] - Cultural Heritage Online Locally, it's often simply referred to as "Kōgyō" (工業) to distinguish it from {{ill|Kagoshima High School|ja|鹿児島高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima High School|1=Kagoshima Academy|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677012}}, known as "Kakō" (鹿高). === Educational Program === * First-year students are grouped by categories, while second and third years are divided into specialized courses ("kei"). * Students choose their specialization at the end of the first year. ; Industrial Category I * Mechatronics * Electrical Engineering * Information Technology * Industrial Chemistry ; Industrial Category II * Architecture * Civil Engineering * Interior Design === School Motto === * Diligence * Creativity * Sincerity == School Song == * Lyrics by Matsujirō Katayama * Music by Kyūhachi Maeda == History == * 1908: Established as Kagoshima District Industrial Apprentice School. * 1919: Renamed Kagoshima Prefectural Industrial School. * 1920: Opening of Kagoshima Prefectural Industrial School; establishment of Mechanical, Architectural, and Furniture departments. * 1925: Furniture Department abolished. * 1927: Renamed Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Industrial School. * 1939: Electrical Department established. * 1948: Renamed Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima High School First Division following educational reforms. * 1949: Reorganized as Kagoshima Technical High School. (Additional historical details follow the same pattern.) == Club Activities == ; Baseball * Achieved its first appearance at [[:en:Koshien|Koshien]] in 2006, reaching the semifinals. ; Soccer * Participated in the [[:en:All Japan High School Soccer Tournament|All Japan High School Soccer Tournament]] four times. ; Rugby * Eleven-time participant in the [[:en:National High School Rugby Tournament|National High School Rugby Tournament]]. ; Archery * Won the national archery tournament in 2007. == Access == * [[:en:Kagoshima City Transportation Bureau|Kagoshima City Transportation Bureau]] Bus No. 5 - "Kōgyō Kōkō-mae" bus stop. * "Gokoku Jinja-mae" bus stop served by city and regional buses. == Notable Alumni == ; Politics * Toshio Sueyoshi - Former Mayor of Kagoshima ; Sports * Munenori Kawasaki - Former professional baseball player (SoftBank Hawks, MLB) * Yūsaku Kuwazuru - Japanese national rugby player (Additional alumni listed accordingly.) == References == == External Links == * {{Official website}} * [http://rokkohob-org.easy-magic.com/user/ Alumni Association Website] * [http://rrfc.jp/ Rugby Club Alumni Association] * [http://rokkou-boxing.com/ Boxing Club Alumni Association] {{Navboxes |title= Club Achievements |list1= {{National High School Archery Tournament Boys' Champions}} {{National High School Archery Tournament Girls' Champions}} }} {{translated page|ja|鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校|version=102422747|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Technical high schools in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[Category:High Schools in National Soccer Championship]] [[Category:High Schools in National Rugby Championship]] [[Category:Participating schools in the All-Japan High School Rugby Selection Tournament]] [[Category:Participating schools in the All-Japan High School Baseball Championship]] [[Category:Participating schools in the Boys Division of the All-Japan High School Ekiden Championship]] [[Category:Technical high schools in Japan]] [[Category:High Schools in the National High School Baseball Championship]] [[Category:Public High Schools in Kagoshima City]] [[ja:鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校]] {{wikidata link|Q11676827}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

Its symbol is a large brick chimney built in 1920, featured in the school song and designated as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Property|1=Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579194}} by the [[:en:Agency for Cultural Affairs|Agency for Cultural Affairs]] in 2004.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/117074 Kagoshima Technical High School Large Chimney] - Cultural Heritage Online Locally, it's often simply referred to as "Kōgyō" (工業) to distinguish it from {{ill|Kagoshima High School|ja|鹿児島高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima High School|1=Kagoshima Academy|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677012}}, known as "Kakō" (鹿高). === Educational Program === * First-year students are grouped by categories, while second and third years are divided into specialized courses ("kei"). * Students choose their specialization at the end of the first year. ; Industrial Category I * Mechatronics * Electrical Engineering * Information Technology * Industrial Chemistry ; Industrial Category II * Architecture * Civil Engineering * Interior Design === School Motto === * Diligence * Creativity * Sincerity == School Song == * Lyrics by Matsujirō Katayama * Music by Kyūhachi Maeda == History == * 1908: Established as Kagoshima District Industrial Apprentice School. * 1919: Renamed Kagoshima Prefectural Industrial School. * 1920: Opening of Kagoshima Prefectural Industrial School; establishment of Mechanical, Architectural, and Furniture departments. * 1925: Furniture Department abolished. * 1927: Renamed Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Industrial School. * 1939: Electrical Department established. * 1948: Renamed Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima High School First Division following educational reforms. * 1949: Reorganized as Kagoshima Technical High School. (Additional historical details follow the same pattern.) == Club Activities == ; Baseball * Achieved its first appearance at [[:en:Koshien|Koshien]] in 2006, reaching the semifinals. ; Soccer * Participated in the [[:en:All Japan High School Soccer Tournament|All Japan High School Soccer Tournament]] four times. ; Rugby * Eleven-time participant in the [[:en:National High School Rugby Tournament|National High School Rugby Tournament]]. ; Archery * Won the national archery tournament in 2007. == Access == * [[:en:Kagoshima City Transportation Bureau|Kagoshima City Transportation Bureau]] Bus No. 5 - "Kōgyō Kōkō-mae" bus stop. * "Gokoku Jinja-mae" bus stop served by city and regional buses. == Notable Alumni == ; Politics * Toshio Sueyoshi - Former Mayor of Kagoshima ; Sports * Munenori Kawasaki - Former professional baseball player (SoftBank Hawks, MLB) * Yūsaku Kuwazuru - Japanese national rugby player (Additional alumni listed accordingly.) == References == == External Links == * {{Official website}} * [http://rokkohob-org.easy-magic.com/user/ Alumni Association Website] * [http://rrfc.jp/ Rugby Club Alumni Association] * [http://rokkou-boxing.com/ Boxing Club Alumni Association] {{Navboxes |title= Club Achievements |list1= {{National High School Archery Tournament Boys' Champions}} {{National High School Archery Tournament Girls' Champions}} }} {{translated page|ja|鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校|version=102422747|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Technical high schools in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[Category:High Schools in National Soccer Championship]] [[Category:High Schools in National Rugby Championship]] [[Category:Participating schools in the All-Japan High School Rugby Selection Tournament]] [[Category:Participating schools in the All-Japan High School Baseball Championship]] [[Category:Participating schools in the Boys Division of the All-Japan High School Ekiden Championship]] [[Category:Technical high schools in Japan]] [[Category:High Schools in the National High School Baseball Championship]] [[Category:Public High Schools in Kagoshima City]] [[ja:鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校]] {{wikidata link|Q11676827}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Its symbol is a large brick chimney built in 1920, featured in the school song and designated as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Property|1=Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579194}} by the [[:en:Agency for Cultural Affairs|Agency for Cultural Affairs]] in 2004.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/117074 Kagoshima Technical High School Large Chimney] - Cultural Heritage Online Locally, it's often simply referred to as "Kōgyō" (工業) to distinguish it from {{ill|Kagoshima High School|ja|鹿児島高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima High School|1=Kagoshima Academy|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677012}}, known as "Kakō" (鹿高). === Educational Program === * First-year students are grouped by categories, while second and third years are divided into specialized courses ("kei"). * Students choose their specialization at the end of the first year. ; Industrial Category I * Mechatronics * Electrical Engineering * Information Technology * Industrial Chemistry ; Industrial Category II * Architecture * Civil Engineering * Interior Design === School Motto === * Diligence * Creativity * Sincerity == School Song == * Lyrics by Matsujirō Katayama * Music by Kyūhachi Maeda == History == * 1908: Established as Kagoshima District Industrial Apprentice School. * 1919: Renamed Kagoshima Prefectural Industrial School. * 1920: Opening of Kagoshima Prefectural Industrial School; establishment of Mechanical, Architectural, and Furniture departments. * 1925: Furniture Department abolished. * 1927: Renamed Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Industrial School. * 1939: Electrical Department established. * 1948: Renamed Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima High School First Division following educational reforms. * 1949: Reorganized as Kagoshima Technical High School. (Additional historical details follow the same pattern.) == Club Activities == ; Baseball * Achieved its first appearance at [[:en:Koshien|Koshien]] in 2006, reaching the semifinals. ; Soccer * Participated in the [[:en:All Japan High School Soccer Tournament|All Japan High School Soccer Tournament]] four times. ; Rugby * Eleven-time participant in the [[:en:National High School Rugby Tournament|National High School Rugby Tournament]]. ; Archery * Won the national archery tournament in 2007. == Access == * [[:en:Kagoshima City Transportation Bureau|Kagoshima City Transportation Bureau]] Bus No. 5 - "Kōgyō Kōkō-mae" bus stop. * "Gokoku Jinja-mae" bus stop served by city and regional buses. == Notable Alumni == ; Politics * Toshio Sueyoshi - Former Mayor of Kagoshima ; Sports * Munenori Kawasaki - Former professional baseball player (SoftBank Hawks, MLB) * Yūsaku Kuwazuru - Japanese national rugby player (Additional alumni listed accordingly.) == References == == External Links == * {{Official website}} * [http://rokkohob-org.easy-magic.com/user/ Alumni Association Website] * [http://rrfc.jp/ Rugby Club Alumni Association] * [http://rokkou-boxing.com/ Boxing Club Alumni Association] {{Navboxes |title= Club Achievements |list1= {{National High School Archery Tournament Boys' Champions}} {{National High School Archery Tournament Girls' Champions}} }} {{translated page|ja|鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校|version=102422747|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Technical high schools in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[Category:High Schools in National Soccer Championship]] [[Category:High Schools in National Rugby Championship]] [[Category:Participating schools in the All-Japan High School Rugby Selection Tournament]] [[Category:Participating schools in the All-Japan High School Baseball Championship]] [[Category:Participating schools in the Boys Division of the All-Japan High School Ekiden Championship]] [[Category:Technical high schools in Japan]] [[Category:High Schools in the National High School Baseball Championship]] [[Category:Public High Schools in Kagoshima City]] [[ja:鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校]] {{wikidata link|Q11676827}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

Its symbol is a large brick chimney built in 1920, featured in the school song and designated as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Property|1=Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579194}} by the [[:en:Agency for Cultural Affairs|Agency for Cultural Affairs]] in 2004.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/117074 Kagoshima Technical High School Large Chimney] - Cultural Heritage Online Locally, it's often simply referred to as "Kōgyō" (工業) to distinguish it from {{ill|Kagoshima High School|ja|鹿児島高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima High School|1=Kagoshima Academy|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677012}}, known as "Kakō" (鹿高). === Educational Program === * First-year students are grouped by categories, while second and third years are divided into specialized courses ("kei"). * Students choose their specialization at the end of the first year. ; Industrial Category I * Mechatronics * Electrical Engineering * Information Technology * Industrial Chemistry ; Industrial Category II * Architecture * Civil Engineering * Interior Design === School Motto === * Diligence * Creativity * Sincerity == School Song == * Lyrics by Matsujirō Katayama * Music by Kyūhachi Maeda == History == * 1908: Established as Kagoshima District Industrial Apprentice School. * 1919: Renamed Kagoshima Prefectural Industrial School. * 1920: Opening of Kagoshima Prefectural Industrial School; establishment of Mechanical, Architectural, and Furniture departments. * 1925: Furniture Department abolished. * 1927: Renamed Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Industrial School. * 1939: Electrical Department established. * 1948: Renamed Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima High School First Division following educational reforms. * 1949: Reorganized as Kagoshima Technical High School. (Additional historical details follow the same pattern.) == Club Activities == ; Baseball * Achieved its first appearance at [[:en:Koshien|Koshien]] in 2006, reaching the semifinals. ; Soccer * Participated in the [[:en:All Japan High School Soccer Tournament|All Japan High School Soccer Tournament]] four times. ; Rugby * Eleven-time participant in the [[:en:National High School Rugby Tournament|National High School Rugby Tournament]]. ; Archery * Won the national archery tournament in 2007. == Access == * [[:en:Kagoshima City Transportation Bureau|Kagoshima City Transportation Bureau]] Bus No. 5 - "Kōgyō Kōkō-mae" bus stop. * "Gokoku Jinja-mae" bus stop served by city and regional buses. == Notable Alumni == ; Politics * Toshio Sueyoshi - Former Mayor of Kagoshima ; Sports * Munenori Kawasaki - Former professional baseball player (SoftBank Hawks, MLB) * Yūsaku Kuwazuru - Japanese national rugby player (Additional alumni listed accordingly.) == References == == External Links == * {{Official website}} * [http://rokkohob-org.easy-magic.com/user/ Alumni Association Website] * [http://rrfc.jp/ Rugby Club Alumni Association] * [http://rokkou-boxing.com/ Boxing Club Alumni Association] {{Navboxes |title= Club Achievements |list1= {{National High School Archery Tournament Boys' Champions}} {{National High School Archery Tournament Girls' Champions}} }} {{translated page|ja|鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校|version=102422747|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校]] {{wikidata link|Q11676827}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:High Schools in National Rugby Championship]] [[Category:High Schools in National Soccer Championship]] [[Category:High Schools in the National High School Baseball Championship]] [[Category:Participating schools in the All-Japan High School Baseball Championship]] [[Category:Participating schools in the All-Japan High School Rugby Selection Tournament]] [[Category:Participating schools in the Boys Division of the All-Japan High School Ekiden Championship]] [[Category:Public High Schools in Kagoshima City]] [[Category:Technical high schools in Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

Kagoshima Technical High School | type = {{ill|State school|en|State school|WD=Q1080794|lt=Public school}} | founder = Kagoshima Prefecture | established = 1908 | motto = Diligence, Creativity, Sincerity | gender = {{ill|Mixed-sex education|en|Mixed-sex education|WD=Q541394|lt=Co-educational}} | curriculum = {{ill|Full-time education|ja|高等学校#全日制|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Full-time education|1=Japanese high school|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56351315}} | system = Year-based | departments = See below | school_code = 46107F | postalcode = 890-0014 | location = 2-57-1 Kusamuta, Kagoshima City, Kagoshima Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|31|36|30.5|N|130|32|18.4|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | website = {{Official website}} }} [[File:Dai-entotsu-kagoshima-kougyo.jpg|200px|thumb|right|Large Chimney]] '''Kagoshima Technical High School''' (鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校, ''Kagoshima Kenritsu Kagoshima Kōgyō Kōtō Gakkō'') is a {{ill|prefectural|ja|県立|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/prefectural|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 公立|1=prefectural|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392117}} {{ill|Higher Technical Examination Programme|en|Higher Technical Examination Programme|WD=Q3817741|lt=technical high school}} located in {{ill|Kagoshima|en|Kagoshima|WD=Q15674|lt=Kagoshima City}}, Kagoshima Prefecture. Commonly known as '''Rokkō''' (鹿工). == Overview == Founded in 1908 as Kagoshima District Industrial Apprentice School. Like many technical high schools in Japan, it is co-educational, though about 90% of the students are male, with over 20,000 alumni. Its symbol is a large brick chimney built in 1920, featured in the school song and designated as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Property|1=Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579194}} by the {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en|Agency for Cultural Affairs|WD=Q1009519|lt=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} in 2004.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/117074 Kagoshima Technical High School Large Chimney] - Cultural Heritage Online Locally, it's often simply referred to as "Kōgyō" (工業) to distinguish it from {{ill|Kagoshima High School|ja|鹿児島高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima High School|1=Kagoshima Academy|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677012}}, known as "Kakō" (鹿高). === Educational Program === * First-year students are grouped by categories, while second and third years are divided into specialized courses ("kei"). * Students choose their specialization at the end of the first year. ; Industrial Category I * Mechatronics * Electrical Engineering * Information Technology * Industrial Chemistry ; Industrial Category II * Architecture * Civil Engineering * Interior Design === School Motto === * Diligence * Creativity * Sincerity == School Song == * Lyrics by Matsujirō Katayama * Music by Kyūhachi Maeda == History == * 1908: Established as Kagoshima District Industrial Apprentice School. * 1919: Renamed Kagoshima Prefectural Industrial School. * 1920: Opening of Kagoshima Prefectural Industrial School; establishment of Mechanical, Architectural, and Furniture departments. * 1925: Furniture Department abolished. * 1927: Renamed Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Industrial School. * 1939: Electrical Department established. * 1948: Renamed Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima High School First Division following educational reforms. * 1949: Reorganized as Kagoshima Technical High School. (Additional historical details follow the same pattern.) == Club Activities == ; Baseball * Achieved its first appearance at {{ill|Koshien|en|Koshien|WD=Q841854|lt=Koshien}} in 2006, reaching the semifinals. ; Soccer * Participated in the {{ill|All Japan High School Soccer Tournament|en|All Japan High School Soccer Tournament|WD=Q4729027|lt=All Japan High School Soccer Tournament}} four times. ; Rugby * Eleven-time participant in the {{ill|National High School Rugby Tournament|en|National High School Rugby Tournament|WD=Q6973073|lt=National High School Rugby Tournament}}. ; Archery * Won the national archery tournament in 2007. == Access == * {{ill|Kagoshima City Transportation Bureau|en|Kagoshima City Transportation Bureau|WD=Q3537114|lt=Kagoshima City Transportation Bureau}} Bus No. 5 - "Kōgyō Kōkō-mae" bus stop. * "Gokoku Jinja-mae" bus stop served by city and regional buses. == Notable Alumni == ; Politics * Toshio Sueyoshi - Former Mayor of Kagoshima ; Sports * Munenori Kawasaki - Former professional baseball player (SoftBank Hawks, MLB) * Yūsaku Kuwazuru - Japanese national rugby player (Additional alumni listed accordingly.) == References == == External Links == * {{Official website}} * [http://rokkohob-org.easy-magic.com/user/ Alumni Association Website] * [http://rrfc.jp/ Rugby Club Alumni Association] * [http://rokkou-boxing.com/ Boxing Club Alumni Association] {{Navboxes |title= Club Achievements |list1= {{National High School Archery Tournament Boys' Champions}} {{National High School Archery Tournament Girls' Champions}} }} {{translated page|ja|鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校|version=102422747|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校]] {{wikidata link|Q11676827}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:High Schools in National Rugby Championship]] [[Category:High Schools in National Soccer Championship]] [[Category:High Schools in the National High School Baseball Championship]] [[Category:Participating schools in the All-Japan High School Baseball Championship]] [[Category:Participating schools in the All-Japan High School Rugby Selection Tournament]] [[Category:Participating schools in the Boys Division of the All-Japan High School Ekiden Championship]] [[Category:Public High Schools in Kagoshima City]] [[Category:Technical high schools in Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

Kagoshima Technical High School | type = {{ill|State school|en|State school|WD=Q1080794|lt=Public school|1=State school}} | founder = Kagoshima Prefecture | established = 1908 | motto = Diligence, Creativity, Sincerity | gender = {{ill|Mixed-sex education|en|Mixed-sex education|WD=Q541394|lt=Co-educational|1=Mixed-sex education}} | curriculum = {{ill|Full-time education|ja|高等学校#全日制|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Full-time education|1=Japanese high school|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56351315}} | system = Year-based | departments = See below | school_code = 46107F | postalcode = 890-0014 | location = 2-57-1 Kusamuta, Kagoshima City, Kagoshima Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|31|36|30.5|N|130|32|18.4|E|region:JP-46_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | website = {{Official website}} }} [[File:Dai-entotsu-kagoshima-kougyo.jpg|200px|thumb|right|Large Chimney]] '''Kagoshima Technical High School''' (鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校, ''Kagoshima Kenritsu Kagoshima Kōgyō Kōtō Gakkō'') is a {{ill|prefectural|ja|県立|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/prefectural|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 公立|1=Q11392117|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392117}} {{ill|Higher Technical Examination Programme|en|Higher Technical Examination Programme|WD=Q3817741|lt=technical high school|1=Higher Technical Examination Programme}} located in {{ill|Kagoshima|en|Kagoshima|WD=Q15674|lt=Kagoshima City|1=Kagoshima}}, Kagoshima Prefecture. Commonly known as '''Rokkō''' (鹿工). == Overview == Founded in 1908 as Kagoshima District Industrial Apprentice School. Like many technical high schools in Japan, it is co-educational, though about 90% of the students are male, with over 20,000 alumni. Its symbol is a large brick chimney built in 1920, featured in the school song and designated as a {{ill|Registered Tangible Cultural Property|ja|登録有形文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Registered Tangible Cultural Property|1=Registered Tangible Cultural Property of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11579194}} by the {{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en|Agency for Cultural Affairs|WD=Q1009519|lt=Agency for Cultural Affairs|1=Agency for Cultural Affairs}} in 2004.[https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/117074 Kagoshima Technical High School Large Chimney] - Cultural Heritage Online Locally, it's often simply referred to as "Kōgyō" (工業) to distinguish it from {{ill|Kagoshima High School|ja|鹿児島高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagoshima High School|1=Kagoshima High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677012}}, known as "Kakō" (鹿高). === Educational Program === * First-year students are grouped by categories, while second and third years are divided into specialized courses ("kei"). * Students choose their specialization at the end of the first year. ; Industrial Category I * Mechatronics * Electrical Engineering * Information Technology * Industrial Chemistry ; Industrial Category II * Architecture * Civil Engineering * Interior Design === School Motto === * Diligence * Creativity * Sincerity == School Song == * Lyrics by Matsujirō Katayama * Music by Kyūhachi Maeda == History == * 1908: Established as Kagoshima District Industrial Apprentice School. * 1919: Renamed Kagoshima Prefectural Industrial School. * 1920: Opening of Kagoshima Prefectural Industrial School; establishment of Mechanical, Architectural, and Furniture departments. * 1925: Furniture Department abolished. * 1927: Renamed Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima Industrial School. * 1939: Electrical Department established. * 1948: Renamed Kagoshima Prefectural Kagoshima High School First Division following educational reforms. * 1949: Reorganized as Kagoshima Technical High School. (Additional historical details follow the same pattern.) == Club Activities == ; Baseball * Achieved its first appearance at {{ill|Koshien|en|Koshien|WD=Q841854|lt=Koshien|1=Koshien}} in 2006, reaching the semifinals. ; Soccer * Participated in the {{ill|All Japan High School Soccer Tournament|en|All Japan High School Soccer Tournament|WD=Q4729027|lt=All Japan High School Soccer Tournament|1=All Japan High School Soccer Tournament}} four times. ; Rugby * Eleven-time participant in the {{ill|National High School Rugby Tournament|en|National High School Rugby Tournament|WD=Q6973073|lt=National High School Rugby Tournament|1=National High School Rugby Tournament}}. ; Archery * Won the national archery tournament in 2007. == Access == * {{ill|Kagoshima City Transportation Bureau|en|Kagoshima City Transportation Bureau|WD=Q3537114|lt=Kagoshima City Transportation Bureau|1=Kagoshima City Transportation Bureau}} Bus No. 5 - "Kōgyō Kōkō-mae" bus stop. * "Gokoku Jinja-mae" bus stop served by city and regional buses. == Notable Alumni == ; Politics * Toshio Sueyoshi - Former Mayor of Kagoshima ; Sports * Munenori Kawasaki - Former professional baseball player (SoftBank Hawks, MLB) * Yūsaku Kuwazuru - Japanese national rugby player (Additional alumni listed accordingly.) == References == == External Links == * {{Official website}} * [http://rokkohob-org.easy-magic.com/user/ Alumni Association Website] * [http://rrfc.jp/ Rugby Club Alumni Association] * [http://rokkou-boxing.com/ Boxing Club Alumni Association] {{Navboxes |title= Club Achievements |list1= {{National High School Archery Tournament Boys' Champions}} {{National High School Archery Tournament Girls' Champions}} }} {{translated page|ja|鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校|version=102422747|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:鹿児島県立鹿児島工業高等学校]] {{wikidata link|Q11676827}} [[Category:High Schools in National Rugby Championship]] [[Category:High Schools in National Soccer Championship]] [[Category:High Schools in the National High School Baseball Championship]] [[Category:Participating schools in the All-Japan High School Baseball Championship]] [[Category:Participating schools in the All-Japan High School Rugby Selection Tournament]] [[Category:Participating schools in the Boys Division of the All-Japan High School Ekiden Championship]] [[Category:Public High Schools in Kagoshima City]] [[Category:Technical high schools in Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
